Package org.eventb.core.ast.datatype

Interface IDatatypeBuilder

All Known Implementing Classes:
DatatypeBuilder
public interface IDatatypeBuilder
Common protocol for constructing new datatypes. To create a new datatype, you must first obtain an instance of this interface from a formula factory, then declare all constructors of the datatype and finalize it.

New instances can be obtained by calling FormulaFactory.makeDatatypeBuilder(String, java.util.List) or FormulaFactory.makeDatatypeBuilder(String, GivenType...) or FormulaFactory.makeDatatypeBuilder(String, java.util.List, Object) providing it with the datatype name and type parameters.

Then datatype constructors can be added with the addConstructor(String) method and their arguments could be added directly on the IConstructorBuilder objects returned.

Once all constructors have been added, with at least one basic constructor (which does not reference the datatype itself), the method finalizeDatatype() must be called to obtain the final datatype. The restriction on the existence of a basic constructor ensures that the built datatype will not be empty (all types are inhabited in Event-B).

A parser that can parse string representations of argument types referencing the type parameters or the datatype itself is provided. It can be used to define easily constructors argument types (see IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(Type)).

Since:
3.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    IConstructorBuilder
    addConstructor(String name)
    Adds a new constructor to the datatype.
    IDatatype
    finalizeDatatype()
    Finalizes this builder and returns the assembled datatype.
    FormulaFactory
    getFactory()
    Returns the base formula factory of this builder, that is the formula factory that created this instance.
    boolean
    hasBasicConstructor()
    Tells if the datatype has a basic constructor.
    IParseResult
    parseType(String strType)
    Provides a parser that can parse a string representation of a type referencing type parameters or the datatype type itself.

  • Method Details

    • addConstructor

      IConstructorBuilder addConstructor(String name)
      Adds a new constructor to the datatype. The arguments of the constructor are then defined through the returned object. The constructor name must be an identifier name different from all names already declared in the datatype (i.e., the datatype name, the name of already added constructors and destructors). However, the given name can be the same as one of the formal type parameters of the datatype.
      Parameters:
      name - the constructor name
      Returns:
      an IConstructorBuilder for which arguments could be defined
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given name is not an identifier name or is not fresh
      IllegalStateException - if this IDatatypeBuilder has already been finalized

    • getFactory

      FormulaFactory getFactory()
      Returns the base formula factory of this builder, that is the formula factory that created this instance.
      Returns:
      the base formula factory

    • parseType

      IParseResult parseType(String strType)
      Provides a parser that can parse a string representation of a type referencing type parameters or the datatype type itself. It returns the correct intermediate representation necessary to add constructor arguments with IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(Type) or IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(String, Type).

      A datatype type must only be written as DT(X,Y,Z) with DT the datatype name and (X,Y,Z) the type parameters in the exact same order they were declared in the datatype (X first type parameter, etc.).

      If the parse result was correctly parsed (i.e. not IResult.hasProblem()) then a call to IParseResult.getParsedType() will provide a type in the correct intermediate representation of type that must be used for constructor arguments.

      Parameters:
      strType - the string representation of a constructor argument type
      Returns:
      the parse result in the correct intermediate representation for constructor argument type
      See Also:
      • IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(Type)
      • IConstructorBuilder.addArgument(String, Type)

    • hasBasicConstructor

      boolean hasBasicConstructor()
      Tells if the datatype has a basic constructor. A basic constructor is a constructor that does not reference the datatype itself.
      Returns:
      true iff a basic constructor has already been defined

    • finalizeDatatype

      IDatatype finalizeDatatype()
      Finalizes this builder and returns the assembled datatype.

      At least one basic constructor must have been added to this builder.

      Upon return from this method, this datatype builder and all constructor builders that it created are finalized and cannot be used anymore.

      Any subsequent call will return the same datatype object as the first successful call.

      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the datatype does not have a basic constructor
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • hasBasicConstructor()
